subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF, OECD, Eurostat, RSF, World Justice Project, EIOPA, EEA, ECtHR) + Greek official (ELSTAT, Bank of Greece) + Greek/EU press.

The fiscal turnaround (primary surpluses, regained investment grade) is the standout positive; the lowest press freedom in the EU, severe climate exposure, and demographics are the standout negatives.
Source-lean: press-freedom and migration factors leaned partly on rights-NGO/Anglophone framing, corroborated by RSF and European-court rulings.

economic.notes: A post-crisis turnaround: large primary surpluses, fast-falling (if still high) debt, recapitalized banks, and a world-leading merchant fleet.

fiscal_state.notes: A post-crisis turnaround: large primary surpluses, debt high (~150% GDP) but falling fast, and regained investment-grade ratings (2023-24). (IMF Art IV; DBRS)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB is credible and independent; no national control. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Reaccelerated to ~4.6% YoY (Apr 2026) on housing/utilities; expected to converge to target long-term. (Eurostat; ECB)

banking_stability.notes: Four systemic banks recapitalized; NPLs down to ~3.3% (from crisis peaks above 40%), CET1 ~15%, returning to profit. (Bank of Greece FSR 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A world-leading merchant fleet and record tourism, plus exports; stable EU-delegated policy; no resource curse; a goods-trade deficit the offset. (INSETE; UNCTAD)

institutional.notes: Credible (post-reform) statistics and improving digital government, against weak rule of law and the lowest press freedom in the EU.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP ~48/143, weak on order and security; the Predator surveillance scandal and the Tempi train-disaster accountability crisis are live concerns. (WJP 2025; HRW 2026)

statistical_integrity.notes: ELSTAT has been independent and ESS-compliant since the post-2010 reforms, with consecutive Eurostat validations; the historical 'Greek statistics' problem is resolved. (Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: EU-leading digitalization via gov.gr and RRF funds, against a deep clientelism legacy. (OECD 2025)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ranks Greece low for the EU (89th, 2025): journalist surveillance, the unsolved Karaivaz murder, and ownership concentration. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A stable single-party majority under protest pressure, with lawful investor residency, against steep demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: A single-party majority that survived a censure motion, but huge Tempi-disaster protests and recurring general strikes. Stable but pressured. (Al Jazeera; Euronews)

civil_liberties.notes: Speech and assembly broadly protected, but the surveillance scandal, police-violence findings, and asylum-access suspensions erode the floor. (WJP; HRW 2026)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, durable routes (a Golden Visa with 2024 threshold increases; a digital-nomad visa), with friction at the asylum margin. (EY)

social_cohesion.notes: A migration frontline: a 2025 European-court ruling against systematic pushbacks and an asylum-access suspension; strained, not fractured. (ECtHR; Amnesty)

demographics.notes: Severe: fertility ~1.32, births down sharply, a shrinking and ageing population, and crisis-era brain drain of mostly young emigrants. (ELSTAT)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable NATO/EU member, with structural Turkey tensions as a recurring negative-skew tail.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO and committed EU member with high defence spending and deepening regional partnerships. (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: Structural tensions with Turkey (airspace, the Aegean, Cyprus, the eastern Mediterranean), periodically acute amid a fragile detente; no active conflict, but a negative-skew tail. (FDD)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU sanctions participant; the 2015 capital controls were fully lifted in 2019. (EC; Bank of Greece)

physical_practical.notes: Low crime and improving (EU-funded) infrastructure, against severe and worsening climate exposure and an austerity-hollowed health system.

climate.notes: A Mediterranean front line - extreme heat, chronic drought, and catastrophic wildfire (2023/24), lower readiness. (IPCC AR6; Germanwatch CRI; EEA)

healthcare.notes: The universal ESY system was hollowed by austerity: high unmet need, low satisfaction, high out-of-pocket costs, and staffing gaps. (OECD Health Profile 2025)

infrastructure.notes: Improving via RRF/EU funds and digital leadership, but the Tempi disaster exposed deep rail-safety neglect. Net improving. (EC)

crime_safety.notes: Low violent crime, mostly petty theft in tourist hubs; the crime index has fallen for years. (GPI 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: Among the EU's highest protection gaps - residential flood and earthquake cover very low against severe wildfire/flood/seismic exposure. (EIOPA)

summary: A striking post-crisis fiscal turnaround - large primary surpluses, fast-falling (if still high) debt, regained investment grade, recapitalized banks - alongside a world-leading merchant fleet and record tourism. The drags are weak rule of law (a surveillance scandal, the Tempi-disaster accountability crisis), the lowest press freedom in the EU, severe and worsening climate exposure, an austerity-hollowed health system, and steep demographic decline. Tensions with Turkey are a recurring negative-skew tail.
